title: Indexes, Query Plans, Operator Costs, and Responsible Tuning · Data Contract Brief: Selectivity Access Paths
summary: A growing ticket dataset makes queue and analytics queries slow, but each new index adds write and storage cost. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql selectivity access paths increment in an evidence-backed query performance dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Estimate selectivity and compare scans, seeks, lookups, and join access paths from data distribution.
Order composite keys from predicates and sorting needs and identify prefix and covering behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern

A defensible solution applies Selectivity Access Paths, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an evidence-backed query performance dossier.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-selectivity-access-paths — Estimate selectivity and compare scans, seeks, lookups, and join access paths from data distribution.
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT ticket_id, owner_id, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status = 'open' AND severity IN ('high', 'critical')
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tickets_queue
  ON tickets(status, severity, opened_at, ticket_id);
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
